expectations.text: Identifies SQL injection risk from interpolating tableName into SELECT * FROM {tableName}
expectations.evidence: Critical Issue #3: 'SQL injection risk via table name interpolation... Use proper identifier escaping or better yet, avoid the query entirely by reading files from disk.'

expectations.text: Flags missing authentication/authorization (user must own the project)
expectations.evidence: Critical Issue #2: 'No authentication... The entire dataset router has no auth middleware. This export endpoint would allow unauthenticated bulk data extraction.'

expectations.text: Identifies missing limits (row count, dataset count, or memory/size constraints)
expectations.evidence: Critical Issue #4: 'Memory exhaustion / no row limits... SELECT * with no LIMIT against large tables will OOM the Node process.'

expectations.text: Flags GET with query string as problematic for many IDs (URL length limits, should use POST)
expectations.evidence: Not mentioned. The review suggests validating/limiting dataset IDs but does not flag the fundamental issue of GET query string length limits or recommend POST.

expectations.text: Mentions error handling for non-existent or inaccessible dataset IDs
expectations.evidence: Minor suggestion #14: 'Return a 404 or 400 (not 500) when no valid datasets are found.' Also mentions partial failure handling in Reviewer 3.

expectations.text: Review is structured with clear priority levels (critical vs minor)
expectations.evidence: Explicit 'Critical Issues', 'Strong Recommendations', 'Minor Suggestions', and 'Alternative Approaches' sections.
